mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces aggregate demand forecasts, causing equity risk premiums to rise and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.1 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvements, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >50% of recovery periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating inflation compresses real yields and forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a negative Rank IC or fails to predict negative returns in >45% of tightening months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports wage-driven consumption, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in >55% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.5%, economic contraction fears trigger immediate equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP readings below 1.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained regimes, increases in industrial production signal capacity expansion that alleviates cost pressures, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of supply-constrained periods, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
